song.title: Glorious Things of Thee Are Spoken
song.lyrics:
[V1] 1) Glorious things of thee are spoken, Zion, city of our God! He whose word cannot be broken Formed thee for His own abode;|| On the Rock of Ages founded, What can shake thy sure repose? With salvation’s walls surrounded, Thou may’st smile at all thy foes.
[v2] 2) See the streams of living waters, Springing from eternal love, Well supply thy sons and daughters, And all fear of want remove;|| Who can faint, when such a river Ever flows their thirst t’assauge? Grace which, like the Lord, the giver, Never fails from age to age.
[V3] 3) Blest constituents of Zion, Washed in the Redeemer’s blood! Jesus, whom their souls rely on, Makes them kings and priests to God.|| ’Tis His love His people raises Over self to reign as kings: And as priests, His worthy praises, Each his thankful off’ring brings.
[V4] 4) Savior, if of Zion’s city I, through grace, a member am; Let the world deride or pity, I will glory in Thy name.|| Fading is the worldling’s pleasure, All his boasted pomp and show; Solid joys and lasting treasure, None but Zion’s children know.
song.author: John Newton
